skill.name: lark-approval
skill.description: Lark Approval API integration for managing approval instances and tasks through the lark-cli command-line tool.
skill.summary: Manage Lark approval workflows, instances, and tasks via CLI commands
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' and 'filesystem' patterns are markdown code blocks containing CLI documentation examples, not executable code. This skill contains only documentation with bash command examples for the lark-cli tool. No actual code execution, filesystem access, or security risks present.

content.user_title: Manage Lark Approval Workflows via CLI
content.value_statement: Streamline Lark approval workflow management through Claude Code integration. Query approval instances, approve or reject tasks, and transfer approvals using natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: Query approval instance details and status
Approve, reject, or transfer approval tasks
Add CC recipients to approval instances
List and query user approval tasks
View API parameter schemas before execution
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed on the system
Requires proper Lark API authentication and permissions
Cannot create new approval definitions or workflows
Depends on lark-shared skill for authentication setup
Only supports v4 approval API endpoints

use_cases.title: Manager Approval Automation
use_cases.description: Managers can quickly review, approve, or reject pending approval requests by describing what they want in natural language

use_cases.title: Approval Workflow Monitoring
use_cases.description: Operations teams can query approval status, check pending tasks, and track approval instances across the organization

use_cases.title: Batch Approval Processing
use_cases.description: Process multiple approval tasks efficiently by querying task lists and performing bulk approve/reject operations

prompt_templates.title: Check Pending Approvals
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all my pending approval tasks that need attention
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of approvals awaiting your action

prompt_templates.title: Approve a Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Approve the approval task with instance ID [instance_id] and task ID [task_id]

prompt_templates.title: Get Approval Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the full details of approval instance [instance_id] including current status and history

prompt_templates.title: Transfer Approval Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Transfer approval task [task_id] to user [user_id] with a note about the handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Delegating approval responsibility to another team member

output_examples.input: Show me my pending approval tasks
output_examples.output:
Found 3 pending approval tasks:
1. Leave Request - John Doe (2024-01-15 to 2024-01-17)
2. Expense Report $850 - Jane Smith
3. Purchase Order #12345 - Tech Team

Would you like to approve or reject any of these?

output_examples.input: Get details for approval instance ABC-123-XYZ
Approve task TASK-789 for instance ABC-123-XYZ
output_examples.output: Task approved successfully. The approval instance ABC-123-XYZ has advanced to the next stage in the workflow.

best_practices: Always run the schema command first to check required parameters before calling approval APIs
Use the shared authentication setup from lark-shared skill to ensure proper API credentials
Verify the required approval scopes (approval:instance:read, approval:task:write) are granted before execution
Check approval status before performing actions to avoid errors on already-completed workflows
anti_patterns: Do not guess parameter structures - always use lark-cli schema approval.resource.method to verify required fields
Avoid approving or rejecting tasks without first reviewing the full instance details and context
Do not transfer approval tasks without informing the recipient and documenting the reason
Never hardcode approval instance IDs or user IDs in prompts - retrieve them dynamically

faq.question: What permissions do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need appropriate Lark API scopes: approval:instance:read for viewing, approval:instance:write for modifying instances, and approval:task:read or approval:task:write for task operations. These must be configured in your Lark app credentials.

faq.question: Why do I get parameter errors when calling approval APIs?
faq.answer: You must run 'lark-cli schema approval.resource.method' before each API call to see the exact parameter structure. The API requires specific JSON formats for --data and --params flags.

faq.question: Can I create new approval workflows with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill only manages existing approval instances and tasks. Creating new approval definitions or workflows requires using the Lark Admin API or web console separately.

faq.question: How do I handle approval errors or failed operations?
faq.answer: Check the lark-cli error output for specific error codes. Common issues include insufficient permissions, invalid instance IDs, or trying to act on already-completed approvals. Verify instance status first.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Lark approval types?
faq.answer: This skill works with Lark v4 approval API which supports most standard approval types. Custom approval workflows with specialized fields may require additional parameters beyond the standard schema.

faq.question: What happens when I transfer an approval task?
faq.answer: The task is reassigned to the specified user, who becomes responsible for the approval decision. The original approver no longer has access to approve that task. The transfer is logged in the approval history.
